name: cloudflare-browser
title: Cloudflare Browser
description: Render, screenshot, and extract data from any URL on Cloudflare's edge.
author: tuxr

preferences.name: apiToken
preferences.title: API Token
preferences.description: Token with Browser Rendering: Edit permission.

commands.name: screenshot
commands.title: Screenshot URL
commands.subtitle: Cloudflare Browser
commands.description: Capture a screenshot of any URL on Cloudflare's edge.

commands.name: pdf
commands.title: Export PDF
commands.subtitle: Cloudflare Browser
commands.description: Export any URL as a PDF document.

commands.name: markdown
commands.title: Extract Markdown
commands.subtitle: Cloudflare Browser
commands.description: Extract clean Markdown from any webpage.

commands.name: links
commands.title: Extract Links
commands.subtitle: Cloudflare Browser
commands.description: Extract every link from a webpage, including hostnames.

commands.name: analyze
commands.title: Analyze URL
commands.subtitle: Cloudflare Browser
commands.description: Analyze a URL for phishing, brand impersonation, and security risks using AI. Requires Raycast Pro.

tools.name: screenshot
tools.title: Screenshot URL
tools.description: Capture a PNG screenshot of a URL on Cloudflare's edge. Returns the local file path. Use when the user asks to see what a page looks like.

tools.name: extract-markdown
tools.title: Extract Markdown
tools.description: Render a webpage and return its content as clean Markdown. Use when the user asks about the textual content of a URL or wants to summarize, search, or analyze a page.

tools.name: extract-links
tools.title: Extract Links
tools.description: List every link on a webpage with hostnames. Use for outbound-domain audits or to enumerate sublinks on an index page.

tools.name: analyze
tools.title: Analyze URL
tools.description: Run an AI security and phishing analysis on a URL. Returns risk level (low/medium/high), reasoning, brand impersonation status, login form detection, and a list of suspicious indicators. Use whenever a user asks if a URL is safe, suspicious, or phishing.

ai.instructions: You help users render, screenshot, and analyze URLs safely via Cloudflare Browser Run. The user's Cloudflare credentials never leave their machine. Tool selection rules: for security questions ('is this safe?', 'is this phishing?', 'check this link'), always use analyze. For 'what does this page say?' or 'summarize this URL', use extract-markdown. For 'what does this page link to?' or outbound-domain audits, use extract-links. For 'what does this page look like?' or visual evidence, use screenshot. Always pass fully-qualified http(s) URLs; do not invent URLs the user did not provide.
